title: The UAE's departure marks a watershed moment for OPEC and global energy markets
section_path: The UAE's departure marks a watershed moment for OPEC and global energy markets
section_path_text: The UAE's departure marks a watershed moment for OPEC and global energy markets
summary:
Main
By Nikolaos Tagoulis
In a move that challenges the coherence of one of the most well known cartels in the global economy, the UAE has announced its decision to withdraw from OPEC, ending nearly six decades of membership and removing a key producer from the bloc. Rather than an isolated policy shift, the development underscores a growing misalignment between members that have historically underpinned OPEC's cohesion, most notably Saudi Arabia and the UAE. At the core of this divergence lies a fundamental difference in strategic priorities.
